zh.lifeSummary: 當MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE於1095年出生在英國時，他的父親Manasser De Aguillon17歲，母親Eleanor de Marmion-21歲。他和Isabel De Aguillon至少有1個兒子。他去世於1156年享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE于1095年出生在英国时，他的父亲Manasser De Aguillon17岁，母亲Eleanor de Marmion-21岁。他和Isabel De Aguillon至少有1个儿子。他去世于1156年享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: AGUILLON
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Jesus, Pablo, Juan, Agustin, Carlos, Ernesto, Manuel, Roberto, Ruben, Santiago.
Spanish (Aguillón) and French (Occitan):
habitational name from any of several places called Aguillón in Spain (Asturias or Galicia), or Aguillon in Var, France.

nameMeanings.name: ATTE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Cecile, Gervais, Jean Pierre, Jean-Claude, Leonce, Pierre.
South German: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow, from Middle High German matte ‘meadow’.
German: from a short form of the personal name Matthias or Matthäus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Matter
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived by a meadow, from Matte 1 + -er, suffix denoting an inhabitant.
English (Lancashire); Dutch (also De Matter): occupational name for a maker of mats, from an agent derivative of Middle English matte, Middle Dutch mat ‘mat’.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: STONE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

variants.name: Stoner
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stones
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stenner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt.

German: occupational name for a stonemason or stonecutter, or a topographic name for someone who lived on stony ground, from a derivative of Middle High German stein ‘stone’, ‘stony place’. Compare Stone and Stein .
English: variant of   Stayner  .

variants.name: Clougherty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Clochartaigh ‘descendant of Clochartach’, a personal name, probably formed with cloch ‘stone’, Stone being an alternative Anglicized form of the surname.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Stoneman
meanings: English: perhaps an occupational name for someone who was the servant (Middle English man) of a person surnamed Stone . Alternatively, the name may have been an alias of Stone , used for someone who lived by a prominent stone or rock or at a minor place so named (Middle English ston + man).
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name for someone who worked with stone or who sold precious stones; a jeweler.

English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Ston(e)man (Old English Stān(e)mann), a pet form of Old English personal names beginning with Stān- (from the word ‘stone’, ‘rock’) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -mann.

nameMeanings.name: MANASSER
meanings: Hebrew: Forgetfulness

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE was born in 1095, in England, his father, Manasser De Aguillon, was 17 and his mother, Eleanor de Marmion, was -21. He had at least 1 son with Isabel De Aguillon. He died in 1156, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE est né en 1095, à Angleterre, son père, Manasser De Aguillon, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Marmion, avait -21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Isabel De Aguillon. Il est décédé en 1156, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE 1095 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Manasser De Aguillon 17 und seine Mutter Eleanor de Marmion -21. Er hatte mit Isabel De Aguillon mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1156 im Alter von 61 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE è nato il nel 1095 nel England, il suo padre, Manasser De Aguillon, aveva 17 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor de Marmion, aveva -21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Isabel De Aguillon. Morì nel 1156, all'età di 61 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE(이)가 1095년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Manasser De Aguillon(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor de Marmion(은)는 -21세였다. 그는 Isabel De Aguillon(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1156년 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE nasceu em 1095, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Manasser De Aguillon, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor de Marmion, tinha -21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Isabel De Aguillon. Ele faleceu em 1156, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE родился в 1095 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Manasser De Aguillon было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Marmion, -21. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Isabel De Aguillon. Он умер в 1156 г. в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando MANASSER DE AGUILLON  ATTE STONE nació en 1095, en Inglaterra, su papá, Manasser De Aguillon, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Eleanor de Marmion, tenía -21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Isabel De Aguillon. Murió en 1156, a la edad de 61 años.
